Starting intravenous fluids will quickly address the dehydration, especially important in children where rapid correction is crucial; however, given the absence of clear bacterial infection indicators like fever or bloody stool, broad-spectrum antibiotics aren’t necessarily indicated unless other signs suggest an infectious cause.
